lifecycles._note: COVERAGE-ONLY, heavy (multi-node GPU cluster nodes are billable + slow). Closes ALL 9 uncovered compute/multinodegpucluster WRITE endpoints WITHOUT provisioning a real GPU node/cluster-fabric. No catalog GPU lifecycle exists; bodies are docs-derived (data/api_docs.json models gpunodecreaterequest / gpunodedetailsrequest / clusterfabricdetailsrequest / gpunodeoperationrequest / gpunodeterminaterequest / gpunodeassignpublicnatiprequest / clusterfabricmembermodifyrequestbody) — there are NO data/api_bodies.json entries for this service. UNPROVEN. Cost/safety pattern (proven by compute-virtualserver vs-server-action-coverage): {gpu_node_id} is declared via a NO-OP soft-capture selector that deliberately finds nothing, so at runtime it stays LITERAL ('{gpu_node_id}') -> per-id URLs carry a non-existent literal id and 404 (never touch a real node), while the catalog normalizes {..} to '*' so every endpoint is RECORDED (covered). create POST /v1/gpu-nodes + bulk DELETE /v1/gpu-nodes + start/stop + modify-members use SYNTHETIC vpc/subnet/image/server-type/cluster-fabric/node-pool ids we do not own -> guaranteed 4xx, so NO real billable GPU node is ever created or deleted (hence no capture/cleanup). Every write is optional + grouped + broad expect_status [200,201,202,204,400,403,404,409,422] so a 4xx/404 never fails the run. ENABLED+heavy: runs only under SCP_RUN_HEAVY=true AND SCP_ALLOW_MUTATIONS=true; the destructive deletes additionally need SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE=true.

steps.name: seed-gpu-node-placeholders
steps._note: COST/SAFETY: declares {gpu_node_id} via a NO-OP soft-capture selector that finds nothing, so it stays LITERAL at runtime -> per-id writes 404 (never mutate a real GPU node). The catalog still normalizes {..} to '*' so the endpoints are RECORDED. List array key is gpu_nodes (docs gpunodelistresponse).

steps.name: create-gpu-nodes
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs gpunodecreaterequest + gpunodedetailsrequest + clusterfabricdetailsrequest). POST /v1/gpu-nodes provisions GPU nodes into a cluster-fabric/node-pool. Uses SYNTHETIC vpc/subnet/image/server-type/cluster-fabric/node-pool ids we do not own -> guaranteed 4xx, so NO real billable node is created (no capture/cleanup). Required: cluster_fabric_details{cluster_fabric_name,node_pool_id}, image_id, os_user_id, os_user_password, region_id, server_details[]{gpu_node_name,nat_enabled,server_type_id}, subnet_id, tags, vpc_id.

steps.name: start-gpu-nodes
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs gpunodeoperationrequest -> gpu_node_ids). POST /v1/gpu-nodes/start powers on a list of GPU nodes. Synthetic id -> 4xx; still records.

steps.name: stop-gpu-nodes
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs gpunodeoperationrequest, same shape as start). POST /v1/gpu-nodes/stop powers off a list of GPU nodes. Synthetic id -> 4xx; still records.

steps.name: modify-cluster-fabric-members
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs clusterfabricmembermodifyrequestbody). POST /v1/cluster-fabrics/modify-members moves GPU nodes from before_cluster_fabric_id to after_cluster_fabric_id. Synthetic fabric + node ids -> 4xx; still records.

steps.name: lock-gpu-node
steps._note: PUT /v1/gpu-nodes/{gpu_node_id}/lock enables delete-protection. Docs declare NO request body -> empty body. Literal {gpu_node_id} -> 404; still records. Paired with unlock.

steps.name: unlock-gpu-node
steps._note: PUT /v1/gpu-nodes/{gpu_node_id}/unlock disables delete-protection. Docs declare NO request body. Literal {gpu_node_id} -> 404; still records.

steps.name: assign-gpu-node-public-nat-ip
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs gpunodeassignpublicnatiprequest). POST /v1/gpu-nodes/{gpu_node_id}/public-nat-ip assigns a public NAT IP. Synthetic public_ip_address_id + literal {gpu_node_id} -> 4xx/404; still records.

steps.name: release-gpu-node-public-nat-ip
steps._note: DELETE /v1/gpu-nodes/{gpu_node_id}/public-nat-ip releases the public NAT IP (singular path, unlike baremetal's plural). Docs declare NO request body. Literal {gpu_node_id} -> 404; still records. Marked destructive but releases nothing on a non-existent id.

steps.name: delete-gpu-nodes
steps._note: UNPROVEN body (docs gpunodeterminaterequest -> gpu_node_ids). DELETE /v1/gpu-nodes (bulk terminate, no path id) takes a list of ids in the body. SYNTHETIC id -> guaranteed 4xx, so NO real node is terminated. Marked destructive (needs SCP_ALLOW_DESTRUCTIVE). Recorded regardless of status.
